{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]] involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|qq=|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|qq=|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|qq=|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|qq=|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|qq=|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55521285]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I think it is something like "manager of things" as the etymology but not sure and I do not have a source on that yet[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]] involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|qq=|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|qq=|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|qq=|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|qq=|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|qq=|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55521285]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I think it is something like "manager of things" as the etymology but not sure and I do not have a source on that yet[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]] involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]] involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] to [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55521285]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I think it is something like "manager of things" as the etymology but not sure and I do not have a source on that yet[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]] involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]* (Vol. 1), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994. * *Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Vol. 1)*, [[Kodansha Academic Bunko]], translated by {{ill|Takashi Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Ujitani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988. * *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*: Complete Translation of Japan's Classics Vol. 1, [[Shogakukan]], 1983. * *Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary*, p. 947, edited by {{ill|Kōju Takayanagi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōju Takayanagi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and [[:en:Rizō Takeuchi|Risan Takeuchi]], [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1974. * *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, p. 1132, supervised by {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1999.
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]

The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55521285]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I think it is something like "manager of things" as the etymology but not sure and I do not have a source on that yet[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]] involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55521285]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I think it is something like "manager of things" as the etymology but not sure and I do not have a source on that yet[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]] involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]] involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55521285]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I think it is something like "manager of things" as the etymology but not sure and I do not have a source on that yet[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient [[:en:Japan|Japan]] involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the [[:en:Taika Reform|Taika Reforms]]. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]], descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe [[:en:Neck|Kashira]], descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

The Mononobe {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira}}, descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]]
{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}}, who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms}}. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Shugokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Atai (kabane)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Kasahara Sukune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi}}, descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira}}, descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}* (Vol. 1), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994. * *Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Vol. 1)*, [[Kodansha Academic Bunko]], translated by {{ill|Takashi Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Ujitani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988. * *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}*: Complete Translation of Japan's Classics Vol. 1, [[Shogakukan]], 1983. * *Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary*, p. 947, edited by {{ill|Kōju Takayanagi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōju Takayanagi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and {{ill|Rizō Takeuchi|en|Rizō Takeuchi|WD=Q4262779|lt=Risan Takeuchi}}, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|WD=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1974. * *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, p. 1132, supervised by {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1999.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]]
{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}, who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|WD=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms|1=Taika Reform}}. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Q11419535|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Q11581705|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Q27920650|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}, descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|WD=Q9633|lt=Kashira|1=Neck}}, descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604425}}.
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}* (Vol. 1), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994. * *Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Vol. 1)*, [[Kodansha Academic Bunko]], translated by {{ill|Takashi Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Ujitani|1=Ujitani Mitsuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988. * *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}*: Complete Translation of Japan's Classics Vol. 1, [[Shogakukan]], 1983. * *Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary*, p. 947, edited by {{ill|Kōju Takayanagi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōju Takayanagi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670539}} and {{ill|Rizō Takeuchi|en|Rizō Takeuchi|WD=Q4262779|lt=Risan Takeuchi|1=Rizō Takeuchi}}, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|WD=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1974. * *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, p. 1132, supervised by {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549910}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten|1=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1999.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:物部]]
{{Otheruses|Mononobe clan}} '''Mononobe''' (物部) refers to a professional group in ancient {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} involved in military and penal duties under the Imperial Court. It was part of the occupational categories known as [[Shinabe clans|Shinabe]], and members were also assigned to various Ritsuryō-era government offices.
== Overview == The Mononobe had genealogical ties and were historically overseen by the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}, who bore the title of {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomonomiyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} before the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|qid=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms|1=Taika Reform}}. Due to their large numbers, they were referred to as the "Eighty Mononobe Clans" (物部八十氏).
According to the *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}*, in the seventh year of Emperor Sujin's reign, in the eleventh month, Mononobe's ancestor, {{ill|Ikagashiko-o|ja|伊香色雄|1=Ikagashiko-o|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikagashiko-o|qq=}}, was ordered: {{quotation|Let him craft the divine offerings using the eighty flat pots (平瓮, hirabira).<ref>*Nihon Shoki*, Sujin 7th year, 11th month, 13th day entry.</ref>}} "Flat pots" (平瓮, hirabira) refer to flat earthenware vessels resembling plates. A similar description can be found in the middle volume of the *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}*, under the account of Emperor Sujin.
The Mononobe continued to exist after the reforms. During the Ritsuryō period, they were assigned to offices such as the {{ill|Shugokushi|ja|囚獄司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugokushi|1=Q11419535|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11419535}} (40 people), {{ill|Emonfu|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emonfu|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11626338}} (30 people), and the {{ill|Tōzai no Ichishi|ja|東西市司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzai no Ichishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 市司|1=City Offices (Tōji and Sai)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11480073}} (20 people each). They were tasked with carrying out punishments on criminals.
Another occupational group with similar duties was the {{ill|Kumebe|ja|久米部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumebe|qq=}}.
== Mononobe Clan == Many Mononobe clans unrelated to the main {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}} existed in various regions: 1. The Mononobe {{ill|Naohi|ja|直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naohi|1=Q11581705|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581705}}, descended from {{ill|Kasahara Sukune|ja|笠原使主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasahara Sukune|1=Q27920650|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q27920650}}'s son, Ani-maro, part of the {{ill|Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|武蔵国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 无邪志国造}} lineage. 2. The Mononobe {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|qid=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}, descended from {{ill|Iiinune no Mikoto|ja|飯入根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 飯入根|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55533680}}'s son, Unoji-no-Mikoto, of the {{ill|Izumo clan|lt=Izumo clan|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|Izumo Province 造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} lineage. 3. The Mononobe {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}, descended from {{ill|Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja|夏花命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsuhana no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11359471}} lineage (also associated with the {{ill|Isobe clan|ja|磯部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe clan|1=Isobe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11588086}}). 4. The Mononobe {{ill|Neck|en|Neck|qid=Q9633|lt=Kashira|1=Neck}}, descended from Nukata-no-Omi and Musashi-no-Omi, both eighth-generation descendants of {{ill|Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja|米餅搗大使主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yonemochitsuki Taishujin no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 米餅搗大使主|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604425}}.

== References == * *{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}* (Vol. 1), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994. * *Nihon Shoki: Complete Modern Translation (Vol. 1)*, [[Kodansha Academic Bunko]], translated by {{ill|Takashi Ujitani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashi Ujitani|1=Ujitani Mitsuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449517}}, 1988. * *{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}*: Complete Translation of Japan's Classics Vol. 1, [[Shogakukan]], 1983. * *Kadokawa Second Edition Japanese History Dictionary*, p. 947, edited by {{ill|Kōju Takayanagi|ja|高柳光寿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōju Takayanagi|1=Mitsutoshi Takayanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11670539}} and {{ill|Rizō Takeuchi|en|Rizō Takeuchi|qid=Q4262779|lt=Risan Takeuchi|1=Rizō Takeuchi}}, {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|qid=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1974. * *Iwanami Japanese History Dictionary*, p. 1132, supervised by {{ill|Keiji Nagahara|ja|永原慶二|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiji Nagahara|1=Keiji Nagahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549910}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|qid=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten|1=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1999.
